profile_meta_description: Founded in 2016 and headquartered in Berlin, GESTALT Automation GmbH is an industrial integrator for AI-powered Smart Inspection Systems. The company develops and industrializes inline and end-of-line inspection solutions for real-time industrial quality assurance.  A key technological focus lies in the combination of hyperspectral imaging and artificial intelligence. This enables the detection of chemical compositions, contaminations, and hidden defects that cannot be identified using conventional machine vision systems – “See the Unseen.”  GESTALT follows a hybrid AI approach combining rule-based methods, anomaly detection, and deep learning, while integrating multiple sensor technologies into a unified industrial inspection architecture.  Its solutions are deployed across industries including automotive, aerospace, pharmaceuticals, food, energy, and electronics.

profile_meta_description: Digimet Solutions is a Brazilian Industry 4.0 technology company that develops a fully web-based platform for the digitalization and automation of industrial laboratory and quality control processes. profile_meta_description: At ACS Industries, we are the world leader in engineered knitted wire mesh solutions. We were founded as American Copper Sponge in Rhode Island, USA in 1939, where we manufactured knitted copper sponges.  Seven years later, we introduced our first knitted wire mesh air and grease filters.  As a result of the heavy growth in the knitted wire mesh business unit, management changed American Copper Sponge to ACS Industries, Inc. in 1960. Since the 1970s, we expanded our production into automotive exhaust products, and introduced our first knitted wire mesh support systems.   Today, we are the world’s largest vertically integrated knitted wire mesh manufacturer. Additionally, we are a key global supplier of pyrotechnic airbag inflator filters and exhaust systems components to both Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) and Tier 1 suppliers. Furthermore, we are a global supplier to both the foodservice and janitorial industries.

profile_meta_description: Temposonics is a global leader in the field of magnetostrictive measurement technology. For 50 years, we have pioneered the industry, embodying innovation and maximum reliability. Specializing in high-precision linear position sensors and level transmitters, Temposonics offers the broadest range of magnetostrictive sensor solutions in terms of design, output, and performance. Our non-contact sensors provide accurate and dynamic position and velocity measurements for automation and safety-critical systems in industrial machinery, off-highway vehicles, and process industries.  We have manufacturing facilities in Lüdenscheid and Dortmund (Germany) and Cary (NC, USA) with direct sales offices and authorized distributors worldwide. Thanks to our many years of experience and focus on our core technology, based on the principle of magnetostriction, we are always one step ahead of industry trends. Our customers benefit from a combination of application knowledge, service and collaboration.

profile_meta_description: For more than 20 years, Graebener® has been one of the first companies to focus on the research and development of manufacturing processes and machines for fuel cell and electrolyzer components.  The company has dedicated itself to setting the quality standard for manufacturing technologies by developing innovative processes and machines that help to manufacture components, such as the metallic bipolar plate, that are tailor-made, integrable, scalable and thus economically efficient.  For this purpose, Graebener® accompanies all strategically important steps towards the optimally designed production line: Starting from engineering with a view to plate design and line concept via prototyping and small series production in the in-house production laboratory up to the customized production machine or line.   Experience from more than 100 years of metal processing and worldwide customer-specific special solutions are incorporated into the holistic development partnership with the customer.

profile_meta_description: QualityStorm is an AI-driven platform for industrial visual inspection and quality traceability. We help manufacturers replace paper and spreadsheets with digital inspection plans executed on the shopfloor using fixed cameras or mobile devices. In the Training module, teams upload images, label defects/objects, build datasets, and train/validate multiple AI models per product or station. In the Execution module, operators select an inspection plan, capture images, and receive real-time pass/fail decisions with on-image markings. All results are stored with photos, timestamps, and dashboards for audits, CAPA, and continuous improvement. Typical outcomes: fewer defects and rework, faster inspections, standardized routines, and full traceability across plants.

profile_meta_description: ZEISS Industrial Quality Solutions is a leading manufacturer of multidimensional metrology solutions. These include coordinate measuring machines, optical and multisensor systems, microscopy systems for industrial quality assurance as well as metrology software for the automotive, aircraft, mechanical engineering, plastics and medical technology industries. In addition, ZEISS Industrial Quality Solutions offers a broad global spectrum of customer services with ZEISS Quality Excellence Centers close to its customers. The company is headquartered in Oberkochen. Production and development sites outside Germany are located in Minneapolis in the USA, Shanghai (China) and Bangalore (India). company_name: Ruhr-Universität Bochum Lehrstuhl für Mikrosystemtechnik mechIC
profile_meta_description: mechIC develops and distributes sensors and system solutions for monitoring strain, force, and overload, whereby the sensors do not require any power to measure, store, and evaluate data. The first product is a strain sensor that detects mechanical overloads and serves as an “error memory.” Three variants are to be launched on the market: a real-time strain sensor, a semi-active overload sensor, and a passive sensor with integrated memory. The business model is based on the development and distribution of standardized sensor chips and system solutions. In addition to hardware, mechIC offers communication interfaces (e.g., LoRaWAN, Bluetooth, NFC) as well as basic software and an app for reading the sensors. Manufacturing is outsourced to semiconductor foundries, while development, calibration, firmware, and testing are handled by mechIC.
website_home_meta_description: mechIC stands for mechanical Integrated Circuits. mechIC develops sensors that do not require electrical energy for measuring and storing.

profile_meta_description: Aksa Process Engineering designs and manufactures electromagnetic, magnetic, and physical separation technologies for industrial material processing. We develop integrated process systems covering comminution, classification, separation, thermal processing, and bulk material handling within a unified engineering framework. Our scope includes both standalone equipment and turnkey process plants, supported by process engineering, flowsheet development, commissioning, and technical services.  Our solutions are applied across mining, ceramics, iron & steel, recycling, industrial minerals, coal, and food industries. Systems are engineered for defined process conditions to ensure efficient separation, beneficiation, and impurity removal, with a focus on operational reliability and consistent product quality. We provide custom-engineered solutions tailored to specific material characteristics and process requirements.

profile_meta_description: FERESPE is a sand and investment casting company producing technical parts for high-performance applications, whether for assembly lines or maintenance needs. As a bespoke steel foundry, we tailor our services to meet the specific requirements of each client, offering unique solutions that often involve specialized alloy compositions, testing, and certifications. With a strong focus on markets requiring cast components, particularly in the valve industry and sectors such as Energy, Oil & Gas, Clean & Renewable Energy, Water & Wastewater, LNG & Cryogenic, Refining & Petrochemical, Chemical, Railway, Pulp & Paper, Mining, Marine & Shipyard, we export about 90% of our production. Our cast components undergo heat treatment when required and are then processed in specialized workshops for machining, surface treatment, painting, and pre-assembly, before being delivered from our facilities - often directly to the customer’s production line.

profile_meta_description: On September 1st, 1946 the Master machine builder Ludwig Boschert founded our company. Very soon the small company was known for its good quality workmanship and it grew quickly. Shortly thereafter the young company was taken over by the Kunz family. Since then, contacts and friendships have been built up all over the world. As a result, our plant has grown constantly, changing from a small workshop to a large industrial production facility. Boschert punching machines and notchers are known in sheet metal shops worldwide as machines that are built to last. In the field of safety chucks, as well, we have proven our forward-thinking ways with the many international patents we have been awarded for innovative new products. Boschert is a company with a bright future. By staying true to our heritage of innovative products we will continue the success of our past. As satisfied customers have been our incentive in the past, they remain our responsibility for the future.

profile_meta_description: Common Sense Robotics delivers affordable and reliable automation for touch labor in manufacturing. Our AI-powered Task Execution System combines symbolic reasoning with machine learning to transform unstructured work instructions, visuals, and domain knowledge into structured, executable tasks. By enabling robots to understand task intention, adapt to real-world conditions, and execute contact-rich operations with precision, we unlock a new category of automation beyond traditional robotics. The result is increased productivity, improved quality, and scalable deployment across complex industrial environments.

profile_meta_description: Haply Robotics designs and manufactures high fidelity haptic controllers for robotics, simulation, and human machine interaction. Our product family of devices, Inverse3, Inverse 3X, and MinVerse, are engineered for exceptional precision, and responsiveness. These devices deliver accurate motion capture and force feedback, enabling intuitive and stable control for robotic environments.  Built for high bandwidth communication and precise data acquisition, our controllers are ideal for capturing high quality motion and interaction data to train robotic AI models. Their accuracy makes them powerful tools for human guided data collection and advanced learning systems.  Haply Hub, our unified interface for diagnostics and support, and HARP, our robotic teleoperation platform enabling precise human in the loop control across robot systems, extend our hardware capabilities. Haply empowers organizations to integrate the sense of touch into the generation robotic applications worldwide.

profile_meta_description: NanoSen develops next-generation force sensor technologies based on PNC materials in combination with our proprietary force sensing impedance (FSI) technology. Our materials form the basis for highly sensitive force and tactile sensors that are characterized by high signal stability, reliability, and mechanical robustness. By evaluating the frequency-dependent electrical behavior under load, force sensing impedance technology enables precise measurement of force and touch in real time over a wide measuring range.  Our solutions are used in medical technology, automation, and intelligent systems. NanoSen's goal is to provide innovative and scalable force sensor solutions that can be flexibly integrated into existing systems and enable new applications. We supply both standardized products and customer-specific developments – from the selection of suitable components to implementation in prototypes.

profile_meta_description: The Bosch Group is a leading global supplier of technology and services. It employs roughly 412,000 associates worldwide (as of December 31, 2025). According to preliminary figures, the company generated sales of 91 billion euros in 2025. Its operations are divided into four business sectors: Mobility, Industrial Technology, Consumer Goods, and Energy and Building Technology. With its business activities, the company aims to use technology to help shape universal trends such as automation, electrification, digitalization, connectivity, and an orientation to sustainability. In this context, Bosch’s broad diversification across regions and industries strengthens its innovativeness and robustness. Bosch uses its proven expertise in sensor technology, software, and services to offer customers cross-domain solutions from a single source. It also applies its expertise in connectivity and artificial intelligence in order to develop and manufacture user-friendly, sustainable products.

profile_meta_description: Since 1969, Vasconi S.r.l., based in Cuveglio (Varese), has been active in the mass production of metal components through cold stamping, machining, assembly, and tool manufacturing. The company operates automatic presses with capacities of up to 500 tons and manages over 400 dies, with a production capacity exceeding 50 million parts per year.
